danger_signals.visual_form: Boss encounters at stage ends; subordinate enemies throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger from sealed demon threat; each stage represents resistance to Ta-Keed's influence spreading through kingdom

danger_signals.visual_form: Spikes, obstacles, branching paths with deadly terrain
danger_signals.meaning: World corruption or curse manifestation; areas become increasingly hostile as Ta-Keed's power spreads

danger_signals.visual_form: Armored humanoid summoning electrical attacks in palace setting
danger_signals.meaning: Corrupted royal authority or Ta-Keed's control over kingdom's military forces

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct enemy types associated with each character's introductory stage
danger_signals.meaning: Different manifestations of Ta-Keed's corruption across kingdom regions; stage-specific threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Hearts replenishing character health bars; potions fully restoring health
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones or supply points; recovery opportunity within hostile stages

safety_signals.visual_form: Crystal orbs increasing life bar capacity; extra life items
safety_signals.meaning: Empowerment through divine bells' influence; growing strength against Ta-Keed

safety_signals.visual_form: Four distinct playable characters with complementary abilities
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical safety through role adaptation; choosing character suited to current threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Background architecture showing structured kingdom space
safety_signals.meaning: Civilization stronghold; seat of Emperor Hans XIV's protective authority

faction_indicators.faction: The Four Heroes (Samson, Kikira, Gamm, K.O.)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Four bells (divine artifacts of power; distributed among heroes)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hero-specific stage environments; Samson in climbing terrain, Kikira in fire-themed areas, Gamm on spike-laden paths, K.O. in narrow passages

faction_indicators.faction: Kingdom of Forgy / Emperor Hans XIV
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Royal authority; knights and military forces as visual representation
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Lightning-summoning knight boss suggests corrupted royal military
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Palace setting; structured architecture; presence of organized army

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Supernatural/demonic corruption; boss encounters as faction manifestation
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Stage-specific boss designs; each boss represents different subordinate or aspect of Ta-Keed's power
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hazardous terrain; cursed or corrupted stage environments; mountain sealing location

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gods dispersed power as four bells; stable kingdom structure under Emperor Hans VI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kingdom functioned normally; military unprepared for supernatural threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Demon subordinates spread across kingdom; environmental hazards increase; boss encounters emerge
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Emperor Hans XIV summons heroes; royal army proved powerless; world becomes progressively more hostile

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Four heroes complete individual stages; visual confirmation of character empowerment through bells
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Samson appointed leader by Hans XIV; Kikira challenges for dominance then accepts hierarchy; team formation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Linear stage progression through all 20 stages; escalating difficulty and boss complexity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players forced to restart if Samson dies (critical leader); other characters can remain dead until stage completion or revival via potion

environmental_storytelling.technique: Stage-specific hazard design tied to character abilities
environmental_storytelling.example: Spike-covered terrain in Gamm's stage teaches player that golem's spike-walking ability is essential; icy surfaces in Kikira's stage require claw traction for traversal

environmental_storytelling.technique: Boss design as region-specific antagonist manifestation
environmental_storytelling.example: Lightning knight in palace represents Ta-Keed's corruption of royal military; diverse boss types across stages show different aspects of demonic influence
First four stages function as character introductions in relatively controlled environments; later stages feature branching paths and increased hazard density, showing world destabilization
Health restoration items and potions scattered throughout stages indicate resource scarcity in corrupted world; their presence marks temporary sanctuaries

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character death mechanics as world-state messaging
environmental_storytelling.example: Samson's forced restart on death (leader-dependent failure state) versus other characters remaining dead until stage completion communicates hierarchical importance and consequence weight

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Throws bells as projectiles; climbs walls and ceilings; designated team leader by Emperor Hans XIV; death forces restart
Flies for limited duration; charges fire breath; claws provide icy surface traction; initially opposes Samson's leadership but accepts after defeat; remains engaged with team
Slow movement; strong attacks in any direction; walks on spike terrain; quiet acceptance of hierarchy; powerful but deliberate
Fastest character; lowest health; fits through narrow passages; climbs walls/ceilings; drops bombs; compensates for fragility with evasion
Summons heroes in response to Ta-Keed threat; appoints Samson as leader; acts as narrative authority figure mediating team hierarchy
Released by lightning strike; spreads subordinates across kingdom; manifests in boss encounters; serves as ultimate threat motivating entire narrative

notes: Little Samson communicates world-state primarily through character-ability-environment synergy and escalating environmental hazard complexity. The political structure (Emperor → appointed leader Samson → team hierarchy) is conveyed through narrative beats and mechanical consequence (Samson death = game over). Faction identity relies on role-specific visual design rather than color coding. The game's manifestation layer emphasizes that world corruption manifests differently across regions (stage-specific bosses and hazards), and player tactical choice (character switching) becomes a visual metaphor for how civilization adapts to supernatural threats. Regional differences (cleavage removal, background sparkle edits) suggest localization-driven sanitization rather than narrative significance. The password system visually represents world persistence—players can resume mid-crisis, suggesting the kingdom exists in continuous danger state between attempts.
